query: I have a notebook where Cell 4 defines df_clean by dropping NaN rows from df. Cell 4's execution_count is null (I edited it after my last run and forgot to re-execute). Cell 7 (execution_count=5) references df_clean and prints df_clean.describe(). The notebook looks fine when I scroll through — every cell shows output. Audit this for me.
expected_behavior: Flags Cell 4 as unrun (execution_count = null) and identifies that Cell 7 depends on df_clean which Cell 4 defines
Explains that Cell 7's printed output reflects a stale df_clean — the kernel never re-evaluated Cell 4 against its current source, so Cell 7 ran against the previous version's binding
Recommends a kernel restart and full top-to-bottom re-run (Restart Kernel and Run All Cells, or equivalent) rather than just re-running Cell 4 alone
